metadata.title: SYNGO_MF_2495
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The authors show that slow KAR-mediated postsynaptic responses in MF-CA3 synapses are mediated by GluK2/GluK5 heteromers via sensitivity to UBP310, a compound that was first to be selective for GluK1 or GluK3 containing KARs only. Authors show that UBP310 is an antagonist of GluK2/GluK5 heteromers and not of GluK2 homomeric KARs in a heterologous system. Via activity of the UBP310 compound in GluK1 or GluK3 KO animals the action of UBP310 on GluK2/GluK5 heteromers and not GluK1 or GluK3 containing KARs is confirmed.

Note: Of Gluk5 is it known that it is unable to form functional homopentameric KARs. It is known to form GluK2/GluK5 heteropentamers that represent a large portion of all KAR-mediated responses in the mammalian brain.. Experimental description: "Mouse parasagittal brain slices of 14- to 21-day-old C57BL/6 wild-type and GluK1 and GluK3 knockout mice were..."
- Species of the overexpressed GluK2 and Gluk5 proteins is not stated in the paper. Annotation based on the data obtained with the antagonist in vivo.
